title: Endpoint, Mobile, Browser, Wireless, and SOHO Hardening · Fault-Isolation Clinic
summary: A realistic endpoint, mobile, browser, wireless, and soho hardening ticket contains one plausible but causally wrong diagnosis or unsafe action. Find the first unsupported inference and repair the evidence chain. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in layered endpoint hardening and exception register and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inventory exposed services, software, accounts, privileges, defaults, updates, encryption, logs, backups, recovery, accessibility, owner, and exception before hardening.
Choose supported personal or enterprise wireless authentication, encryption, credentials, certificates, onboarding, rotation, revocation, and guest isolation.
Apply screen lock, supported updates, encryption, secure enrollment, app sources, permissions, backups, remote response, connectivity controls, and privacy-aware management.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Hardening Baseline
steps.instruction:
debug case — Browsers, extensions, mobile apps, personal devices, home routers, and public Wi-Fi use defaults with inconsistent updates and recovery. The debug variation 3 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Inventory exposed services, software, accounts, privileges, defaults, updates, encryption, logs, backups, recovery, accessibility, owner, and exception before hardening.

Inspect the Hardening Baseline trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Hardening Baseline.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for inventory exposed services, software, accounts, privileges, defaults, updates, encryption, logs, backups, recovery, accessibility, owner, and exception before hardening. For CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Hardening Baseline, keep Hardening Baseline connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Hardening Baseline, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

Competency probe: Inventory exposed services, software, accounts, privileges, defaults, updates, encryption, logs, backups, recovery, accessibility, owner, and exception before hardening.

stimulus.title: Hardening Baseline case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Browsers, extensions, mobile apps, personal devices, home routers, and public Wi-Fi use defaults with inconsistent updates and recovery. The debug variation 3 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Inventory exposed services, software, accounts, privileges, defaults, updates, encryption, logs, backups, recovery, accessibility, owner, and exception before hardening.

lines.label: risk

lines.label: evidence

Artifact under review: layered endpoint hardening and exception register.

options.text: For inspect the failure record, apply one familiar repair, omit user impact, authorization, safety, asset and version identity, baseline, recent change, competing cause, prediction, observation, rollback, changed-case verification, accessibility, privacy, escalation, and controlled transfer, then accept a missing symptom as support proof.

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Hardening Regression
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Hardening Regression: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Browsers, extensions, mobile apps, personal devices, home routers, and public Wi-Fi use defaults with inconsistent updates and recovery. The debug variation 6 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Test original user tasks, assistive technology, remote and offline access, updates, recovery, logs, exceptions, changed identities, and hostile cases after hardening.

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Hardening Regression.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Hardening Regression, keep Hardening Regression connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test original user tasks, assistive technology, remote and offline access, updates, recovery, logs, exceptions, changed identities, and hostile cases after hardening.
